prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. On October 16, 2014, the United Nations General Assembly, in its 69th session at the United Nations Headquarters in New York City, conducted the 2014 United Nations Security Council election. This electoral process was organized to fill five non-permanent positions on the UN Security Council, with the successful candidates serving two-year terms that would begin on January 1, 2015. Adhering to the established rotation guidelines of the Security Council, which dictate that the ten non-permanent UNSC seats are distributed cyclically among the distinct regional blocs — these blocs being the traditional divisions used by UN member states for electoral and representational functions — the five vacant seats were distributed as follows: one seat was designated for the African bloc, another for the Asia-Pacific Group, a third for the Latin American and Caribbean region, and two seats were allotted to the Western European and Others Group. These five newly elected members are slated to serve on the Security Council for the duration of the 2015–16 term.
B. The 2014 United Nations Security Council election was held on 16 October 2014 during the 69th session of the United Nations General Assembly, held at United Nations Headquarters in New York City. The elections were for five non-permanent seats on the UN Security Council for two-year mandates commencing on 1 January 2015. In accordance with the Security Council's rotation rules, whereby the ten non-permanent UNSC seats rotate among the various regional blocs into which UN member states traditionally divide themselves for voting and representation purposes, the five available seats were allocated as follows: One for Africa One for the Asia-Pacific Group One for Latin America and the Caribbean Two for the Western European and Others GroupThe five members will serve on the Security Council for the 2015–16 period.
C. During the 69th session of the United Nations General Assembly, the 2014 United Nations Security Council election was held at United Nations Headquarters in New York City. The elections were for five non-permanent seats on the UN Security Council for two-year mandates starting from 1 January 2015. The UNSC seats ten non-permanent seats that rotate among various regional blocs. The five seats were distributed as follows: One for Africa One for the Asia-Pacific Group One for Latin America and the Caribbean Two for the Western European and Others Group.

Answer:

options.A: On October 16, 2014, the United Nations General Assembly, in its 69th session at the United Nations Headquarters in New York City, conducted the 2014 United Nations Security Council election. This electoral process was organized to fill five non-permanent positions on the UN Security Council, with the successful candidates serving two-year terms that would begin on January 1, 2015. Adhering to the established rotation guidelines of the Security Council, which dictate that the ten non-permanent UNSC seats are distributed cyclically among the distinct regional blocs — these blocs being the traditional divisions used by UN member states for electoral and representational functions — the five vacant seats were distributed as follows: one seat was designated for the African bloc, another for the Asia-Pacific Group, a third for the Latin American and Caribbean region, and two seats were allotted to the Western European and Others Group. These five newly elected members are slated to serve on the Security Council for the duration of the 2015–16 term.
options.B: The 2014 United Nations Security Council election was held on 16 October 2014 during the 69th session of the United Nations General Assembly, held at United Nations Headquarters in New York City. The elections were for five non-permanent seats on the UN Security Council for two-year mandates commencing on 1 January 2015. In accordance with the Security Council's rotation rules, whereby the ten non-permanent UNSC seats rotate among the various regional blocs into which UN member states traditionally divide themselves for voting and representation purposes, the five available seats were allocated as follows: One for Africa One for the Asia-Pacific Group One for Latin America and the Caribbean Two for the Western European and Others GroupThe five members will serve on the Security Council for the 2015–16 period.
options.C: During the 69th session of the United Nations General Assembly, the 2014 United Nations Security Council election was held at United Nations Headquarters in New York City. The elections were for five non-permanent seats on the UN Security Council for two-year mandates starting from 1 January 2015. The UNSC seats ten non-permanent seats that rotate among various regional blocs. The five seats were distributed as follows: One for Africa One for the Asia-Pacific Group One for Latin America and the Caribbean Two for the Western European and Others Group.
